programme.title: ITV Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service.

programme.title: FYI Extra
programme.desc: Short bulletin rounding up the day's showbusiness stories.

programme.title: ITV Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service.

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot.

programme.title: Late Night Magic
programme.desc: Let Magic be the soundtrack to your late night listening with some of the best tracks of all time.

programme.title: Up All Night! Kiss Beats
programme.desc: Let Kiss keep you up all night with the finest collection of RandB tunes and nocturnal beats.

programme.title: News, Sport, Weather
programme.desc: Round-up of the latest news and sports headlines, plus weather forecasts.

programme.title: News, Sport, Weather
programme.desc: Round-up of the latest news and sports headlines, plus weather forecasts.

programme.title: Sky News
programme.desc: A summary of the latest news headlines.

programme.title: Sky News
programme.desc: A summary of the latest news headlines.

programme.title: Sky News on the Hour
programme.desc: Review of the latest international news, with sport, weather and financial updates.

programme.title: Sky News on the Hour
programme.desc: Review of the latest international news, with sport, weather and financial updates.

programme.title: Sky News
programme.desc: A summary of the latest news headlines.

programme.title: Sky News
programme.desc: A summary of the latest news headlines.

programme.title: Sky News
programme.desc: A summary of the latest news headlines.

programme.title: Sky News
programme.desc: A summary of the latest news headlines.

programme.title: Sky News
programme.desc: A summary of the latest news headlines.

programme.title: Press Preview
programme.desc: A preview of the stories appearing in tomorrow's national newspapers.

programme.title: All The Hits, All Night
programme.desc: Non-stop hits to see you all the way through the night.

programme.title: The Two Ronnies
programme.desc: New World and Tina Charles perform on the vintage comedy and variety show, which includes sketches Patient, Wedding, Spoonerism and Hampton Wick.

programme.title: Underground Worlds
programme.desc: A look at some of Switzerland's 300,000 subterranean bunkers, built across history. Plus, the extraordinary creation of a Liverpool entrepreneur that's shrouded in mystery, and a network of man-made tunnels used by adventurous subterranean thrill-seekers deep beneath Slovenia's landscape.

programme.title: Underground Worlds
programme.desc: The Wieliczka Salt mine in southern Poland, some 327 metres deep, where the voids left behind from excavating salt have been transformed into extraordinary spaces, carved by hand.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home.

programme.title: Ghost Adventures
programme.desc: The ghost hunters find themselves locked in Wyoming Frontier Prison, where they may be among as many as 200 ghosts.

programme.title: Kindred Spirits
programme.desc: Amy Bruni, Adam Berry and Chip Coffey investigate the infamous Harrisville Farmhouse. They call upon the home's original victims to help expose the truth.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home.

programme.title: Pit Bulls & Parolees
programme.desc: Mariah is devastated when she learns that a friend has died. Later, a couple from Tia's past are interested in adopting four dogs.

programme.title: Pit Bulls & Parolees
programme.desc: Hurricane season is approaching and Tia prepares for the worst. Villalobos employee Rico takes on a new duty when his grandparents adopt a shy dog.

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Host Dara O Briain and regulars Hugh Dennis and Andy Parsons are joined by Ed Byrne, Gary Delaney, Katherine Ryan and Josh Widdicombe for an edition of the comedy quiz from September 2014. The panellists give their take on major news stories and participate in a series of stand-up spots and improvised games.

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack are joined by space scientist Maggie Aderin-Pocock, comedian Phil Wang, actor Jason Watkins and EastEnders star Adam Woodyatt, aiming to deceive their opponents with plausible lies. Hosted by Rob Brydon.

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Stephen Fry is joined by guests Sandi Toksvig, John Lloyd, Johnny Vegas and regular panellist Alan Davies for the quiz with a difference. The host asks the teams to consider hypothetical questions, awarding points for the answers he finds the most interesting.

programme.title: Mel Giedroyc: Unforgivable
programme.desc: Mel and Lou are joined by Jimmy Carr, Judi Love and Richard Bacon. Jimmy reveals an act of erotic vandalism, and Judi tells all about the demise of her pet goldfish.

programme.title: Rhod Gilbert's Work Experience
programme.desc: The stand-up comedian tries out being a teacher at Monnow Primary School in Bettws, Gwent. However, 'Mr Gilbert' soon feels the pressure as he tries to impress his colleagues and the children in his class.

programme.title: Eat Your Heart Out with Nick Helm
programme.desc: The comedian and Susan Wokoma arrange to catch up over Austrian Tapas at Kipferl, Mexican tacos from Taquieria and jerk chicken burgers at Boom Burger.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home.

programme.title: Alaska: The Last Frontier
programme.desc: Atz Lee and Eivin travel 160 miles and brave the bears of 'Terrible Island' to hunt deer, while Eve helps Charlotte solve a swarming bee problem.

programme.title: Naked and Afraid XL
programme.desc: Stakes are high as deadly predators close in on the remaining survivalists. But they still need to find food to keep up their energy as they await extraction.

programme.title: Kindig Customs
programme.desc: The 1957 Fairlane build reaches a conclusion as the car is body-worked, painted, assembled and upholstered, and the arrival of the new year bring a new office for the shop foreman.

programme.title: Bear Grylls: Born Survivor
programme.desc: Former Special Forces adventurer Bear Grylls demonstrates how to survive in the harsh Australian outback, including finding food and avoiding sunstroke and saltwater crocodiles.

programme.title: Tanked
programme.desc: Brothers-in-law Wayde and Brett attempt to make a one-of-a-kind DJ-booth aquarium for world-famous artist and DJ Redfoo of the band LMFAO.

programme.title: Barnwood Builders
programme.sub-title: Rebuilding the 100% Cabin and Restoring a Legacy

programme.title: Salvage Dawgs
programme.desc: Mike, Robert and the Black Dog Salvage crew salvage parts of a former navy ship, the USS Zuni/USCG Tamaroa.

programme.title: Wheeler Dealers
programme.desc: Mike restores a rare 1991 Alfa Romeo 164L. They soon find out that although the car has perfect bodywork, it also has a leaking engine.

programme.title: Goblin Works Garage
programme.desc: The team take on a Japanese sports car legend - the Toyota Supra. These cars have been modified countless times, so how can they make theirs stand out?.

programme.title: American Chopper
programme.desc: The team builds a Nascar-inspired motorcycle for the Stewart-Haas racing team, and Paul Jr pitches a line of dog toys to a company based in LA.

programme.title: Fantomworks
programme.desc: Arnold brings Dan two related projects. He wants the team to transform his 1946 Ford Wagon into a classic tour-mobile, and fix the motorcycle that will ride in the back.

programme.title: World's Biggest Shipbuilders
programme.desc: An insight into the work of the elite engineers responsible for constructing a new breed of marine vessel, in some of the world's most expansive shipyards.

programme.title: How It's Made
programme.desc: Investigating how rock crushers, fabric lampshades, cake sprinkles and steam irons are made.

programme.title: How It's Made
programme.desc: A look at where materials come from and how manufacturing processes have developed over the years, featuring detachable IndyCar steering wheels, mixed salad and wind turbines.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri travels to Barcelona, and digs into everything from top-notch tapas, such as prawns and quail, to traditional Catalan specialties including mouthwatering meatballs.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: In Louisville, a funky seafood spot piles their specialties on top of waffles, and in Portland, a killer barbecue food truck stacks a fully-loaded 'shabamwich'.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri samples grilled cheese sandwiches with a twist, made-from-scratch sausages and specialties straight out of Switzerland.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri hunts down all kinds of meaty favourites, starting with an Irish pub's cottage pie in Louisville. Then he grabs burgers at two stops in North Carolina.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy heads to Cancun, Mexico, to dig into cuisine at places like an authentic, family-owned spot where grandma is cooking up rellenos and killer chicharron gorditas.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: In North Carolina, an eatery tucked inside a beer store slings crazy waffle creations, and in Texas, a not-your-everyday fusion joint mashes up barbecue with sushi.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home.

programme.title: Waking the Dead
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. The appeal of a man convicted of murder and mutilation forces Grace to question the decision she made on the case 30 years ago. Someone who appears to be copying his chilling tactics sends the team footage of a new victim having his finger cut off. At the same time, Grace receives threats and a macabre package - a jar of pickled, severed fingers. Sue Johnston and Trevor Eve star.

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: A bereaved couple hire Lovejoy to find a pair of Regency duelling pistols and bring about the downfall of a murderer - but to complete the task he is forced to risk Jane's life and fight a duel. Anthony Valentine guest stars, with Ian McShane.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home.

programme.title: Storage Wars
programme.desc: Darrell and Brandon fight over a piece of movie history, and Rene plans to make the other bidders pay dearly for coming to his home town.

programme.title: Storage Wars
programme.sub-title: Up the Ante in El Monte
programme.desc: Brandi and Jarrod try to outwit their rivals at an auction in El Monte, but face disaster if their plan backfires.

programme.title: Auction Hunters
programme.desc: Ton Jones and Allen Haff run into an old rival at a storage auction, and also acquire a vintage GMC truck and a contraption that could be the first ever Coca-Cola vending machine.

programme.title: Auction Hunters
programme.desc: The traders head to Hollywood, where they try to make a profit by selling a set of vintage Masonic swords, a polygraph machine and a Second World War samurai blade.

programme.title: Mountain Men
programme.desc: Tom takes the fight to the wolves, a lion chase claims a casualty, Morgan builds bigger and Eustace goes to battle to defend his land.

programme.title: Swamp People
programme.desc: Troy pushes his luck, Willie races the clock, and Frenchy goes back to the future. Series following the daily lives of alligator hunters on America's largest swamp.

programme.title: Counting Cars
programme.desc: Part one of two. Danny is hired to do a bumper-to-bumper restoration on a '66 Mustang convertible, and the shop gets a classic Packard in need of some TLC.

programme.title: American Restoration
programme.desc: Rick takes on a huge challenge when a giant slide from the 1960s shaped like a rocket is brought into the shop a piece at a time.

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: The guys race to check out a motorbike once owned by The Great Escape actor Steve McQueen. They also encounter a rare shotgun disguised as a cane.

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: The guys value a first-edition copy of an Ernest Hemingway novel and a 1932 Lincoln Roadster, and discover the story behind an unusual belt-buckle.

programme.title: Forensic Detectives
programme.desc: Two homicides are investigated. The first concerns the murder of a 14-year-old girl in Florida, a crime that bears similarities to a murder in Indiana. The second case sees DNA evidence cause a suspect to confess.

programme.title: Murder on CCTV
programme.desc: The case of Surya Toha, who vanished in March 2010 after dropping her two boys at school in Coral Springs, Florida. Chilling CCTV footage revealed exactly what happened to her.

programme.title: Dr G: Medical Examiner
programme.desc: The case of a man who died almost immediately after flying at his cousin in a violent rage. Plus, the sudden death of a pregnant woman.

programme.title: FBI Files
programme.desc: The hunt for a racially motivated serial killer - a sniper who murdered at least 20 African-American men but left few clues and no witnesses. A countrywide appeal eventually yielded the number of a Kentucky number plate, and led police to white supremacist Joseph Paul Franklin - who promptly escaped custody. He was finally arrested two months later, having made his way to Tampa, Florida.

programme.title: Animal Cops Houston
programme.desc: Officers fight to save a sick and neglected horse.

programme.title: Bondi Vet: Coast to Coast
programme.desc: Dr Kate anxiously awaits her dog's test results. Dr Danni volunteers at a pop-up clinic, while Dr Alex has a house call check-up with a pig and her piglets.

programme.title: Curvy Brides Boutique
programme.desc: Michelle is insecure about her excess skin and dreading seeing herself in a wedding dress. Bride-to-be Lauren opts for a sleeveless gown.

programme.title: Donal MacIntyre's Murder Files
programme.desc: Donal MacIntyre delves into the case of Sylvia and Peter Stuart, with Detective Chief Inspector Andy Guy, to discover how a missing persons case became a murder investigation.

programme.title: Encounters with Evil
programme.desc: Encounters with Evil brings you expert analysis and interviews with those closest to the cases to explore the minds of the perpetrators of the world's most notorious crimes.

programme.title: Donal MacIntyre's Murder Files
programme.desc: Donal MacIntyre delves into the case of Sylvia and Peter Stuart, with Detective Chief Inspector Andy Guy, to discover how a missing persons case became a murder investigation.

programme.title: Encounters with Evil
programme.desc: Encounters with Evil brings you expert analysis and interviews with those closest to the cases to explore the minds of the perpetrators of the world's most notorious crimes.

programme.desc: Find out what's happening in the West Midlands today with all the latest local news, opinions and events.
